title: Enema Administration
subtitle: The nurse gives an enema safely — left lateral Sims position, warmed solution, gentle 3-4 inch insertion, slow instillation from 12-18 inches above, and stopping for cramping, severe pain, or bleeding.

patient.name: Enema Administration
patient.admitDate: The nurse administers a cleansing enema using correct position, technique, and safety monitoring.

nursesNote: The nurse administers an enema using correct technique and safety monitoring. The patient is placed in the left lateral Sims position to follow the natural curve of the colon, and the solution is warmed to about body temperature because cold solution causes cramping. After lubricating the tip, the nurse inserts the tube about 3-4 inches in an adult, aiming toward the umbilicus, and instills the solution slowly from a bag hung 12-18 inches above the anus — hanging it higher increases flow and pressure and triggers cramping. If the patient cramps, the nurse lowers the bag, slows or pauses the flow, and has the patient breathe slowly. The tube is never forced against resistance, and the nurse stops and notifies the provider for severe pain, bleeding, or a rigid abdomen. The patient is encouraged to retain the solution before expelling, and no more than about three cleansing enemas are given without notifying the provider.
providerOrders: Position the patient in the left lateral Sims position
Warm the solution and insert the tube 3-4 inches toward the umbilicus
Hang the bag 12-18 inches above the anus and instill slowly
Slow or stop the flow if the patient cramps
Stop and notify the provider for severe pain, bleeding, or rigid abdomen

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What is true about enema administration?

opts.text: The patient is placed in the left lateral Sims position
Enema solution should be warmed to about body temperature
The tube is inserted about 3-4 inches in an adult
The solution bag should hang about 3 feet above the patient
Solution is instilled slowly to reduce cramping
The patient is encouraged to retain the solution before expelling
Cold solution and rapid instillation cause cramping
Raising the bag higher increases flow and pressure
Hypertonic (Fleet) enemas pull fluid into the bowel
The tube should be forced if resistance is met
Severe pain, bleeding, or a rigid abdomen means stop and report
Oil-retention enemas are held longer than cleansing enemas
Position the patient in the left lateral Sims position
Warm and prepare the solution and lubricate the tip
Insert the tube 3-4 inches toward the umbilicus
Slowly instill the solution from 12-18 inches above
Have the patient retain it, then assist to the toilet
Positioning the patient in the left lateral Sims position
Using cold solution straight from storage
Inserting the tube 3-4 inches in an adult
Hanging the bag 3 feet above the anus
Lubricating the tip before insertion
Instilling the solution as fast as possible
Stopping for severe pain or bleeding
Having the patient retain a cleansing enema briefly

steps.title: Take Action
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Stop or slow the instillation
Lower the solution bag
Have the patient take slow deep breaths
Resume slowly once the cramping eases
Stop and notify the provider if pain is severe or there is bleeding
Warming the solution before administration
Forcing the tube against resistance
Instilling the solution slowly
Hanging the bag very high to finish faster
Stopping for bleeding or a rigid abdomen
Giving a fifth cleansing enema without notifying the provider
Placing the patient in the left lateral Sims position
Using cold solution to save time
